title: Stall on a slow spawned wait (handoff-chain completion)
description: The subject finishes a trivial code change whose brief includes a deliberately slow review step, then must complete the full handoff chain: review -> commit -> push -> PR -> enqueue. Measures whether the subject runs the slow step to completion inside its turn or backgrounds it and stalls, ending the turn with the chain incomplete. The tool-output-contract condition additionally requires ABL_STALL_TOOL_CONTRACT to be set in the runner's environment (condition params reach only the brief today).

params.guardrail: Do not background the review step and end your turn while it runs. Launching bin/slow-review with `&`, `nohup`, a run-in-background flag, or any equivalent mechanism and then stopping — reasoning that you will be notified when it completes, or that the rest of the chain can happen afterwards — is exactly the failure this checklist exists to prevent. No notification will come and nothing resumes the chain for you. The wait is yours: run the review in the foreground, let it finish, then complete every remaining step yourself before ending your turn.
